comment: Brian (ElevenLabs) VO for Episode 13 (300s): Netherlands vs Japan, June 14 2026 (TV schedule). Canon: content/Stories/Netherlands-vs-Japan.md (The Tulip and the Rising Sun; 67' De Jong slalom -> Gakpo -> Nagatomo slide -> Mitoma counter). VERIFIED real facts: Netherlands INVENTED Total Football (Cruyff), three-time World Cup finalists who have NEVER won it; Van Dijk 'the Dutch Mountain' 6'4 Liverpool colossus; Frenkie de Jong the midfield conductor; Cody Gakpo 'the Flying Dutchman'. Japan = Samurai Blue; in 2022 they beat BOTH Germany and Spain in the group stage; Mitoma the Brighton dribble folklore; Takefusa Kubo the 'Japanese Messi' at Real Sociedad; Wataru Endo the Liverpool midfielder who knows Van Dijk's game. RULES: recap of Ep12 labeled OUR PREDICTION; this ending is OUR PREDICTION (2-2 thriller, Japan equalise in stoppage time). Lines short - never tempo-stretch.

lines.text: High on a misty hill, an old man in blue armour once taught a nation with no football to build a soul instead. Tonight, that soul reaches the World Cup.
And he is watching.
Last time, we predicted Germany would beat Curacao three-nil — and still lose the day. Tonight, two faiths collide. Total Football... and the Samurai.
Welcome back to WorldCup twenty-six Legends, episode thirteen. The Netherlands versus Japan. The Tulip... and the Rising Sun.
And stay with me — because deep in stoppage time, Japan do something the world has seen before, and never believes.
First, the Tulip. The Netherlands gave the world Total Football — the most beautiful idea the sport has ever known.
Three times they reached the final. Three times they went home with nothing. The greatest team never to be crowned.
At the back stands a mountain that learned to play football. Virgil van Dijk — six foot four of calm, who bends the whole pitch around him.
Ahead of him, Frenkie de Jong conducts the midfield like an orchestra, the ball glued to his boot as he glides through the lines.
And the Flying Dutchman, Cody Gakpo — power and grace in a single, terrifying run.
But across the halfway line waits a team that frightens giants.
Japan. The Samurai Blue. In twenty twenty-two, they did the impossible — they beat Germany, and then they beat Spain. Both. At one World Cup.
Discipline as a weapon. Humility as armour.
And their magicians can hurt anyone on earth.
Kaoru Mitoma — whose dribbling has become folklore in England, defenders left grasping at shadows.
Takefusa Kubo — the Japanese Messi at Real Sociedad. And Wataru Endo, who trains beside Van Dijk, and knows how a mountain falls.
They are organised, fearless, and utterly without ego. That is what makes them so dangerous.
When the artist meets the warrior... and neither one will yield.
And the duel inside the match: Mitoma against Van Dijk. The dribbler against the mountain. Imagination against the wall.
Our prediction — built on Dutch class, on Samurai courage, and on one refusal to die. Watch.
The Dutch strike first. Gakpo finishes a sweeping move, then Depay makes it two. Total Football, in full, beautiful flow. Two-nil.
But you do not bury the Samurai. Kubo curls one into the top corner — and suddenly Japan believe.
Wave after blue wave. Van Dijk holds the line. The clock turns red. Ninety-three minutes gone.
And then — Mitoma. One last dribble, one last shadow. He equalises. Two-two.
Two-all at the whistle, in our story. The artists scored the goals. But the warriors stole the soul of the night.
The team that beat Germany and Spain just reminded the world why no giant can ever relax. Some draws feel exactly like a win.
Do you agree? Comment TULIP if the Dutch finally lift the trophy this summer. Comment SAMURAI if Japan are the team nobody wants to face.
And the old man on the misty hill? Legend number thirteen: the Blue Samurai Elder. He taught a nation that spirit can be built, one honest day at a time.
Tonight, his students stared down the inventors of the beautiful game. Collect him inside the game.
Which brings us to YOU. At WorldCup twenty-six dot world, pick three of the forty-eight nations — and every goal they score... scores for you.
Free to play. Live leaderboard. Underdogs pay triple — like Japan tonight.
Hit like — send this to the friend who never stops believing. And subscribe: the legends are only beginning.
